description: Language-specific analysis prompts for Scrutin AI code review

securityFocus: SQL Injection via string concatenation in database queries (use parameterized queries or ORM)
Path traversal through unsanitized file path inputs (validate and sanitize paths)
Command injection via Process.Start with user input (avoid shell execution with user input)
XXE vulnerabilities in XML parsing (disable DTD: XmlReaderSettings.DtdProcessing = Prohibit)
XSS in ASP.NET applications (use encoding and HtmlSanitizer)
SSRF when making HTTP requests with user-supplied URLs (validate and whitelist domains)
Hard-coded credentials, API keys, or connection strings (use secrets management / IConfiguration)
Weak cryptographic algorithms (MD5, SHA1, DES) — use SHA256+ and AES
Insecure random number generation (use RandomNumberGenerator, not System.Random for security)
Weak SSL/TLS protocols (enforce TLS 1.2+, disable SSLv3/TLS 1.0)
bugPatterns: Null reference exceptions — missing null checks before member access
Async void methods that swallow exceptions (use async Task instead)
Infinite recursion without base case
Double dispose of IDisposable objects
Race conditions in multi-threaded code without proper locking
Unreachable code after return/throw statements
Collection modified during enumeration
Route parameter type mismatch — controller expects int/Guid but route template allows any string
Missing [FromBody]/[FromQuery]/[FromRoute] attributes causing incorrect model binding
Missing ModelState.IsValid check (or [ApiController] auto-validation not applied)
Missing CancellationToken propagation in async chains (tokens created but not passed downstream)
performancePatterns: HttpClient instantiated per-request causing connection exhaustion — use IHttpClientFactory or a static/singleton HttpClient
HttpClient without timeout — requests may hang indefinitely (set Timeout property or use Polly timeout policy)
DbContext not disposed or scoped incorrectly causing connection pool exhaustion
N+1 queries in Entity Framework (use Include/ThenInclude for eager loading)
Synchronous database calls in async context (use async versions — ToListAsync, FirstOrDefaultAsync)
String concatenation in loops (use StringBuilder)
Boxing/unboxing in hot paths
LINQ queries that enumerate multiple times (materialize with ToList/ToArray once)
Large object allocations in frequently called methods
Missing ConfigureAwait(false) in library code causing deadlocks in sync-over-async contexts
bestPractices: Use records for immutable DTOs
Prefer async/await over Task.Result or .Wait() (causes deadlocks in ASP.NET context)
Use ILogger instead of Console.WriteLine
Follow repository pattern for data access
Use dependency injection instead of new keyword for services
Avoid public fields, use properties
Controllers should delegate to services (thin controllers)

aspnetcore: Missing CSRF protection on state-changing operations
Exposing stack traces in production (disable detailed errors in appsettings.Production.json)
Missing rate limiting on authentication endpoints
Insecure CORS configuration (avoid wildcard origins with credentials)
entityframework: Missing transaction for multiple related operations
Tracking queries when read-only would suffice (use AsNoTracking())
Missing index hints for frequently filtered columns
Open DbContext longer than a single unit of work

securityFocus: XSS via innerHTML or React dangerous HTML props (use textContent or sanitize with DOMPurify)
Prototype pollution through Object.assign or lodash merge with user input
Hard-coded secrets, API keys, or tokens (use environment variables via process.env)
Dynamic code execution risks (avoid eval and new Function constructor)
Permissive CORS configuration in APIs
Insecure localStorage usage for sensitive data (use httpOnly cookies)
URL manipulation without validation (validate URLs before use)
JWT secrets hard-coded or weak (use strong random secrets, rotate regularly)
bugPatterns: Missing await on async functions (fire-and-forget — errors silently lost)
Ignored Promise rejections (always handle with .catch() or try/catch)
Type assertions (as) bypassing actual type safety
Optional chaining without fallback for required values
Array/object mutation when immutability expected
Strict equality issues (=== vs ==)
Wrong HTTP route path — typo or missing leading slash in API route definition
Missing request body/query parameter validation (use zod, joi, or class-validator)
Type mismatch in API handler — expecting number but JSON sends string (use parseInt/coercion)
fetch/axios without timeout — requests may hang indefinitely (use AbortController with setTimeout)
performancePatterns: Creating new database connection/client per request instead of using a shared connection pool
HTTP client (axios/fetch) not reused — configure a shared instance with base URL and interceptors
Unnecessary re-renders in React (missing memo, useMemo, useCallback for expensive operations)
Large bundle imports (import specific modules instead of entire libraries)
Synchronous operations blocking the event loop
Memory leaks from uncleared intervals/timeouts/subscriptions (cleanup in useEffect return)
bestPractices: Use strict TypeScript configuration (strict: true in tsconfig.json)
Prefer unknown over any type
Use const assertions for literal types
Avoid type assertions, prefer type guards
Use interface for object shapes, type for unions/intersections
Handle all Promise rejections — attach .catch() or use unhandledRejection handler

react: Missing key prop in list rendering (use stable unique keys, not array index)
Missing or incorrect useEffect dependencies (causes stale closures or infinite loops)
State updates on unmounted components (cleanup in useEffect)
Mutating state directly instead of using setState
nextjs: Exposing server secrets to client (NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix leaks to browser bundle)
Missing error boundaries for client components
Improper use of 'use client' directive — pushing too much to client bundle

securityFocus: XSS through DOM manipulation with user input (innerHTML, document.write)
Dynamic code execution risks (avoid eval and new Function constructor)
Hard-coded credentials or API keys
Prototype pollution vulnerabilities (Object.assign, merge functions with user input)
Insecure regular expressions (ReDoS — catastrophic backtracking)
Open redirects with unvalidated URLs
Server-side request forgery (SSRF) when proxying user-supplied URLs without validation
Insecure deserialization of JSON with reviver functions that execute code
Missing Content-Security-Policy headers in Express/Fastify responses
JWT verification bypass — using algorithm 'none' or symmetric key confusion
bugPatterns: Implicit global variables (missing var/let/const)
Callback hell without error handling
Type coercion issues (use strict equality ===)
Empty catch blocks swallowing errors silently
This binding issues in callbacks (use arrow functions or .bind())
Hoisting confusion with var
fetch/axios without timeout or AbortController — connection may hang indefinitely
Missing input type validation before using as number, date, or object key
Event listener not removed on cleanup — causes memory leaks in long-lived applications
Circular dependency between modules causing undefined imports at runtime
performancePatterns: Creating new DB connection per request instead of reusing a connection pool
Synchronous file/IO operations in Node.js blocking the event loop (use async variants)
Memory leaks from closures retaining large objects or event listeners not removed
Excessive DOM manipulation (batch updates, use DocumentFragment)
Blocking operations in main thread
Large JSON.parse/stringify on main thread for big payloads — use streaming or web workers
Not using pagination or cursor-based fetching for large database result sets
bestPractices: Use const by default, let when reassignment needed, avoid var
Use template literals instead of string concatenation
Use arrow functions for callbacks
Use destructuring for cleaner code
Handle all Promise rejections (.catch() or try/catch in async)
Enable strict mode ('use strict')
Use ESLint with a shared config for consistent code style
Validate all external inputs at API boundaries with a schema library (Joi, Zod, Yup)

express: Missing error-handling middleware (4-argument function) — unhandled errors crash the process
Not using helmet() middleware for security headers
Missing rate limiting on public endpoints (use express-rate-limit)
Serving static files without cache headers
node: Unhandled promise rejections crashing the process (listen for unhandledRejection event)
Using process.env without validation — missing required variables cause runtime errors
Child process spawned with shell: true and user input — command injection risk

securityFocus: SQL injection through string formatting in queries (use parameterized queries with %s or ?)
Command injection via subprocess with shell=True (use subprocess.run with a list, never shell=True with user input)
Pickle deserialization of untrusted data (use JSON instead)
Hard-coded credentials or secrets
SSL certificate verification disabled (verify=False in requests)
YAML unsafe load (use yaml.safe_load instead of yaml.load)
Path traversal in file operations
bugPatterns: Mutable default arguments in functions (def f(x=[]) — shared across all calls)
Bare except clauses catching all exceptions including SystemExit and KeyboardInterrupt
Variable shadowing in nested scopes
Late binding closures in loops (lambdas capturing loop variable by reference)
requests library used without timeout parameter — connection may hang indefinitely (add timeout=30)
Database connections not closed or not returned to pool (use context managers or connection pooling)
performancePatterns: String concatenation in loops (use ''.join(list) instead)
Repeated list/dict lookups in hot path (cache in local variable)
Not using generators for large datasets (loading everything into memory at once)
Opening too many files or DB connections without closing them (use 'with' statement)
Global Interpreter Lock bottlenecks — use multiprocessing for CPU-bound tasks
bestPractices: Use type hints (typing module) for all function signatures
Follow PEP 8 style guide
Use context managers (with statement) for all resources — files, DB connections, locks
Prefer list comprehensions over map/filter for readability
Use f-strings for string formatting (Python 3.6+)
Use pathlib instead of os.path for file operations

securityFocus: SQL injection through string concatenation (use db.Query with placeholders $1 or ?)
Command injection via exec.Command with shell-interpreted user input
Hard-coded credentials or secrets in source code
Insecure TLS configuration (InsecureSkipVerify: true — never in production)
Path traversal in file handling (sanitize with filepath.Clean and validate prefix)
Race conditions in goroutines accessing shared state without mutex
bugPatterns: Ignoring error return values (all errors in Go must be checked)
Goroutine leaks — goroutines started without a way to stop or cancel them
Nil pointer dereference without nil check before access
Data races — shared state accessed from multiple goroutines without sync.Mutex or atomic
Deferred function call inside a loop causing resource exhaustion (defer runs at function end, not loop end)
Integer overflow in calculations
sql.Rows not closed after query — causes DB connection pool exhaustion (use defer rows.Close())
http.Response.Body not closed after reading — causes TCP connection leak (use defer resp.Body.Close())
Context without timeout for external calls — HTTP or DB requests may hang indefinitely
performancePatterns: http.Client created per-request — reuse a shared http.Client with transport configured (connection pooling built-in)
http.Client without Timeout field — requests may hang indefinitely
DB connection pool not configured (db.SetMaxOpenConns, db.SetMaxIdleConns, db.SetConnMaxLifetime)
Excessive allocations in hot paths (use sync.Pool for frequently allocated objects)
Inefficient string building using + in loops (use strings.Builder)
Unbuffered channels causing blocking when buffer would suffice
Excessive goroutine creation — use worker pool pattern with semaphore or errgroup
bestPractices: Always handle errors explicitly — do not use _ for error return values
Use defer for cleanup of resources (files, DB rows, response bodies)
Prefer composition over inheritance (embed structs)
Use context for cancellation and deadlines across goroutine boundaries
Keep functions small and focused
Use interfaces for dependency injection and testability

securityFocus: SQL injection through string concatenation (use PreparedStatement with ?)
XXE vulnerabilities in XML parsing (disable DOCTYPE, set FEATURE_SECURE_PROCESSING)
Deserialization of untrusted data via ObjectInputStream
LDAP injection in directory queries
Hard-coded credentials or secrets
Path traversal in file operations
Weak cryptography (MD5, SHA1, DES) — use SHA-256+ and AES-256
bugPatterns: Null pointer exceptions (missing null checks before member access)
Resource leaks — streams, connections, ResultSet not closed (use try-with-resources)
Equals and hashCode contract violations
ConcurrentModificationException — collection modified during iteration
Infinite loops without exit condition
Missing @Valid/@Validated on controller method parameters (request body not validated)
Route mapping returns wrong HTTP status for error cases (returning 200 on validation error)
performancePatterns: HttpClient instances not reused — create once (static/singleton), reuse across requests
JDBC Connection not closed or not returned to pool — use try-with-resources or connection pool
Connection pool misconfigured — too few connections (HikariCP maxPoolSize too low for load)
Missing transaction rollback on exception — partial writes left in inconsistent state
String concatenation in loops (use StringBuilder)
Boxing/unboxing in loops (use primitive types)
N+1 query problems with JPA/Hibernate (use JOIN FETCH or @BatchSize)
Inefficient collection operations
bestPractices: Use try-with-resources for AutoCloseable (Connection, Stream, ResultSet)
Prefer Optional<T> over null returns from methods
Use final for immutable fields
Follow SOLID principles
Use dependency injection (Spring @Autowired, @Bean)
Write unit tests for all public methods

securityFocus: SQL injection in database queries (use parameterized queries with ? placeholders or ORM bind variables)
Hard-coded credentials, API keys, or secrets in source code (use environment variables or secret managers)
Insecure network configuration (cleartext traffic, disabled certificate validation in OkHttp)
Path traversal vulnerabilities in file operations (validate and sanitize file paths)
Insecure WebView configuration — JavaScript enabled with addJavascriptInterface exposes native methods
Sensitive data stored in SharedPreferences without encryption (use EncryptedSharedPreferences)
Improper SSL pinning implementation — missing or bypassable certificate pinning in OkHttp
Exported Android components (Activity, BroadcastReceiver) without permission restrictions
Weak cryptographic algorithms (MD5, SHA1) — use SHA-256+ for hashing and AES-256 for encryption
bugPatterns: Null safety bypasses (!! operator overuse — use safe call ?. or let {})
Coroutine scope leaks (GlobalScope usage, coroutines not cancelled on lifecycle end)
Late init var used before initialization (causes UninitializedPropertyAccessException)
Type casting without safety (as instead of as? — throws ClassCastException)
Missing timeout in coroutine network or DB calls (use withTimeout {})
Coroutine exception handler not set — unhandled exceptions crash the application
Flow collection in wrong lifecycle state — collect in repeatOnLifecycle for Android UI
Sealed class when branch missing in exhaustive matching — compiler warning but runtime risk
Data class with mutable properties used as Map key — hashCode changes after insertion
Channel not closed after use — receiving coroutine hangs indefinitely
performancePatterns: Creating new OkHttpClient per request — reuse a shared instance with connection pool
Blocking calls on Dispatchers.Main (use Dispatchers.IO for all IO operations)
Unnecessary object allocations in hot paths (use inline functions and value classes)
Inefficient collection operations (prefer sequences for large collections with chained transformations)
Database connections not returned to pool — use use {} extension or try-with-resources pattern
Room database queries on main thread — use suspend functions or Flow for async queries
Excessive Bitmap allocations without recycling — causes OutOfMemoryError on Android
bestPractices: Use data classes for DTOs and value objects
Prefer val over var — immutability by default
Use sealed classes for restricted hierarchies (state machines, result types)
Use extension functions appropriately — avoid polluting types with unrelated extensions
Handle nullability properly with safe calls (?.) and elvis operator (?:)
Use coroutines with structured concurrency (CoroutineScope, supervisorScope)
Prefer Flow over LiveData for reactive streams in new code
Use Kotlin DSL for Gradle build scripts (build.gradle.kts)

android: Activity/Fragment leaks — ViewModel should not hold references to Activity or Context
Missing lifecycle-aware coroutine scope — use viewModelScope or lifecycleScope
Network calls on main thread — causes NetworkOnMainThreadException
Missing runtime permission checks for dangerous permissions (camera, location, storage)
ktor: Missing authentication plugin on protected routes
Not configuring HttpClient engine timeout (connectTimeout, requestTimeout)
Content negotiation not installed — JSON serialization fails silently
spring: Missing @Transactional on service methods that perform multiple database operations
Coroutine controller methods not returning Flow or suspend — blocks reactor thread
Missing validation annotations (@Valid, @NotNull) on request DTOs

securityFocus: SQL injection through string interpolation in ActiveRecord queries (use ? placeholders or Arel)
Command injection via backticks, system(), or Open3 with user input
Mass assignment vulnerabilities in Rails (use strong parameters — permit only expected attrs)
Hard-coded credentials or secrets (use Rails credentials or environment variables)
Unsafe YAML loading (Psych.safe_load instead of YAML.load)
CSRF protection bypass (protect_from_forgery should not be disabled)
Open redirect via redirect_to with user-controlled URL (validate redirect target)
Insecure direct object reference — missing authorization check before accessing records
ERB template rendering user input without escaping (use html_safe only on trusted content)
bugPatterns: Method visibility issues (public methods intended to be private)
Mutable default values in method signatures (frozen string literals prevent some issues)
Exception handling too broad (rescue StandardError vs rescue specific exception)
Missing HTTP timeout on external calls (Net::HTTP, Faraday, HTTParty without timeout)
N+1 query not detected — accessing association in loop without preloading
Symbol to proc (&:method) on nil receiver — causes NoMethodError
Missing return value check on save/update — use save! or check boolean return
Thread-unsafe code in multi-threaded servers (Puma) — shared mutable state without Mutex
Circular require dependencies causing partially loaded constants
performancePatterns: N+1 queries in ActiveRecord (use includes, eager_load, or preload)
Database connections not returned to pool (ActiveRecord connection pool exhaustion)
Inefficient database queries (select * when only few columns needed — use select or pluck)
Memory bloat from loading large ActiveRecord collections (use find_each with batches)
String concatenation in loops — use Array#join or StringIO
Not using counter_cache for has_many count queries — triggers COUNT query each time
Excessive object allocations in hot paths — use frozen string literals and avoid unnecessary intermediate arrays
bestPractices: Follow Ruby style guide (enforce with RuboCop)
Use strong parameters in Rails controllers
Prefer symbols over strings for hash keys
Use service objects for complex business logic
Use frozen_string_literal: true magic comment for performance
Prefer keyword arguments for methods with multiple parameters
Use ActiveRecord scopes for reusable query conditions
Write request specs and model specs with RSpec and FactoryBot

rails: Missing authorization — user can access records they do not own (use Pundit or CanCanCan)
Callbacks (before_save, after_create) with side effects — hard to test and debug
Database migrations without rollback method — deploy cannot be reverted safely
sidekiq: Job arguments not serializable to JSON — complex objects cause serialization errors
Missing retry configuration — failed jobs retry indefinitely by default
Long-running jobs without heartbeat — Sidekiq may kill them after timeout

securityFocus: SQL injection through string concatenation (use PDO prepared statements with ? or :param)
XSS through unescaped output (use htmlspecialchars() or Twig auto-escape)
Command injection via shell execution functions (shell_exec, passthru, system with user input)
File inclusion vulnerabilities (require/include with user-controlled path)
Hard-coded credentials in source code
Insecure session configuration (session.cookie_httponly, session.cookie_secure not set)
Unrestricted file upload (missing MIME type validation and extension whitelist)
Insecure deserialization via unserialize() with user-controlled input (use json_decode instead)
SSRF when making HTTP requests with user-supplied URLs (validate and whitelist allowed hosts)
bugPatterns: Type coercion issues — loose comparison (== instead of ===) causing unexpected matches
Undefined variable usage without isset() check
Reference vs value confusion in arrays (& modifier side effects)
HTTP requests without timeout — file_get_contents or cURL without CURLOPT_TIMEOUT
Missing return type declarations causing unexpected null returns
Error suppression with @ operator hiding real issues
Incorrect use of empty() — returns true for '0' and 0 which may be valid values
Foreach by reference (&$item) without unset after loop — last element mutated unexpectedly
DateTime mutation — DateTime::modify changes the original object (use DateTimeImmutable)
performancePatterns: N+1 queries in database operations (eager load with JOINs or ORM relationships)
Creating new database connection per request instead of using PDO persistent connections or connection pool
Large file processing without streaming (use fgets/fread instead of file_get_contents for large files)
Not using opcode cache (OPcache) in production — every request re-compiles PHP files
Autoloading entire dependency tree when only a subset is needed
Regex compilation in tight loops — compile once and reuse with preg_match on stored pattern
Loading entire database result set into memory — use cursor or chunked iteration
bestPractices: Use strict type declarations (declare(strict_types=1))
Follow PSR-12 coding standards
Use dependency injection and containers (Laravel container, Symfony DI)
Validate all input data with filter_var or a validation library
Use Composer for dependency management with pinned versions
Use PHP static analysis tools (PHPStan, Psalm) at high levels
Prefer DateTimeImmutable over DateTime to avoid mutation bugs
Use enums (PHP 8.1+) instead of class constants for enumerated values

laravel: Missing authorization policy — gates and policies not defined for resource access
Mass assignment vulnerability — $fillable or $guarded not properly configured on Eloquent models
Missing validation in form requests — controller logic handles unvalidated data
Queued jobs without retry/backoff configuration — failed jobs retry indefinitely
symfony: Missing CSRF token validation on forms (use csrf_token() in Twig templates)
Voter not implemented for fine-grained authorization
Service not properly tagged or autowired — dependency injection fails silently

securityFocus: Unsafe blocks with memory safety issues — document invariants and minimize scope
SQL injection in raw queries (use sqlx bind parameters, not format! macro)
Hard-coded credentials or secrets in source code
Integer overflow — use checked_add/checked_mul or saturating arithmetic in critical paths
Unvalidated user input passed to file system operations (path traversal via Path::join with ..)
Command injection via std::process::Command with shell interpolation of user input
Timing side-channel in secret comparison (use constant_time_eq for comparing hashes/tokens)
Unsafe Send/Sync implementations on types with interior mutability — causes data races
Deserialization of untrusted data with serde without size limits — denial of service via large payloads
bugPatterns: Panic in production code via unwrap() or expect() on None/Err (use ? operator or match)
Deadlocks from incorrect lock ordering (always acquire multiple locks in the same order)
Memory leaks through Rc<RefCell<T>> reference cycles (use Weak for back-references)
HTTP client (reqwest) created per-request — build Client once and reuse (connection pooling built-in)
Mutex poisoning not handled — lock() returns Err after a thread panicked while holding the lock
Tokio runtime blocked by synchronous code — use spawn_blocking for CPU-bound or blocking IO
Iterator consumed twice — iterators are single-use in Rust (clone or collect first)
Lifetime elision hiding borrow conflicts — explicit lifetime annotations clarify ownership
Off-by-one errors in slice indexing — prefer .get() with bounds checking over direct indexing
Drop order dependency — struct fields dropped in declaration order, may cause use-after-free in unsafe
performancePatterns: Unnecessary cloning of large data structures (pass references where ownership not needed)
Blocking operations inside async context (use tokio::task::spawn_blocking for CPU-bound work)
Inefficient string operations — use String::with_capacity when size is known
Excessive heap allocations in hot paths (use stack-allocated arrays when size is bounded)
Not using zero-copy deserialization (serde borrow) for large payloads where applicable
HashMap with default hasher in performance-critical code — consider FxHashMap or AHashMap
Excessive Arc<Mutex<T>> contention — consider lock-free data structures or sharding
bestPractices: Use clippy and address all warnings before merging
Prefer ? operator over unwrap/expect in functions that return Result
Use appropriate error types (thiserror for libraries, anyhow for applications)
Document public APIs with doc comments including examples
Write tests including edge cases and error paths
Use #[must_use] on functions where ignoring the return value is likely a bug
Prefer Into/From trait implementations over manual conversion functions
Use cargo fmt and enforce formatting in CI

actixweb: Missing error response type — Handler returning String instead of proper HttpResponse with status code
Shared mutable state without web::Data<Mutex<T>> — causes data races
Missing extractors validation — Path, Query, Json extractors silently reject malformed input
axum: Missing tower middleware layers for timeout, rate limiting, or CORS
State not shared via Extension or State extractor — handler cannot access application state
Router fallback not configured — returns empty 404 without helpful error message
tokio: Blocking the tokio runtime with synchronous IO (use tokio::fs, not std::fs)
Spawned tasks not joined — errors and panics silently dropped
Missing timeout on async operations — use tokio::time::timeout to prevent hanging

securityFocus: Buffer overflows — use strncpy, snprintf; avoid strcpy/sprintf; always check bounds
Format string vulnerabilities — always use format specifiers in printf/scanf
Use after free and double free — set pointers to nullptr after freeing
Integer overflows in arithmetic — check bounds before operations, use safe integer libraries
NULL pointer dereferences — validate pointers before dereferencing
Uninitialized variable usage — always initialize variables at declaration
Memory leaks — every malloc/calloc must have a corresponding free (prefer RAII/smart pointers)
Unsafe string functions — avoid gets(); use fgets() with size limit
Command injection via system() or popen() with user-controlled input — sanitize all input
TOCTOU race conditions in file operations
Return of stack address — never return pointers to local variables
Hard-coded credentials or encryption keys
bugPatterns: Missing break in switch statements causing fall-through
Off-by-one errors in array indexing and loops
Signed/unsigned comparison issues causing incorrect branch logic
Resource leaks — files not closed, sockets not closed, memory not freed on error paths
Dangling pointers after freeing memory
Incorrect use of sizeof (sizeof(ptr) vs sizeof(*ptr))
Infinite loops without proper termination conditions
Stack overflow from deep recursion or large stack allocations
Incorrect error handling — ignoring return values of system calls (open, read, write)
File descriptor or socket exhaustion — not closing on all error paths
performancePatterns: Unnecessary memory copies (use pointers or references instead of passing large objects by value)
Inefficient string concatenation in loops
Repeated calculations that could be cached in a local variable
Poor cache locality in data structures
Unnecessary dynamic allocations in hot paths (prefer stack or pool allocation)
Virtual function calls in performance-critical inner loops
Not using const references for large object parameters
bestPractices: Use RAII for all resource management (files, memory, locks, sockets)
Prefer smart pointers (unique_ptr, shared_ptr) over raw pointers
Use const correctness for function parameters and methods
Initialize all variables at declaration
Check return values of all system calls and allocation functions
Use static analysis tools (cppcheck, clang-static-analyzer, AddressSanitizer)
Prefer std::vector over raw arrays
Use nullptr instead of NULL or 0 for null pointers

securityFocus: Hard-coded credentials, API keys, or tokens in source code (use Keychain or environment config)
Insecure URLSession configuration — disabled certificate validation (never set serverTrustPolicy to .disableEvaluation in production)
Sensitive data logged via print() or os_log — avoid logging PII, passwords, or tokens
Keychain misuse — storing secrets in UserDefaults instead of Keychain (UserDefaults is not encrypted)
Cleartext HTTP traffic (missing App Transport Security enforcement in Info.plist — restrict NSAllowsArbitraryLoads)
SQL injection in SQLite queries via string interpolation (use parameterized queries with ? placeholders)
Jailbreak detection bypass — security checks relying solely on file existence are trivially bypassed
Improper data protection class — sensitive files should use .completeProtection (NSFileProtectionComplete)
WebView JavaScript injection — WKWebView with unrestricted evaluateJavaScript and addScriptMessageHandler allows code execution
Insecure clipboard usage — sensitive data copied to UIPasteboard is accessible by other apps
bugPatterns: Force unwrapping with ! on Optional — use guard let, if let, or ?? instead
Force casting with as! — use as? and handle nil case explicitly
Retain cycles in closures — capture self weakly ([weak self]) in escaping closures
URLSession data tasks without timeout — requests may hang indefinitely (set timeoutIntervalForRequest)
NotificationCenter observer not removed on deinit — causes crashes on deallocated observer
Accessing UIKit/SwiftUI from background thread — all UI updates must be on @MainActor or DispatchQueue.main
Unhandled async Task errors — Tasks that throw are silently dropped if not awaited or caught
Data race in actor-isolated state accessed from nonisolated context (use await for actor method calls)
Codable decoding failure on missing keys without default values — use decodeIfPresent or CodingKeys with defaults
Infinite recursion in computed properties that reference themselves
performancePatterns: URLSession shared instance overused — configure a custom session with timeout and cache policy
Blocking main thread with synchronous network calls (use async/await or completion handlers)
Excessive view re-rendering due to unnecessary @State/@ObservedObject updates in SwiftUI
Large images loaded without resizing/downsampling before display (use CGImageSource with kCGImageSourceThumbnailMaxPixelSize)
Memory leaks from strong reference cycles in delegate patterns (declare delegates as weak var)
Creating DateFormatter or NumberFormatter inside loops — cache formatters as static properties
Excessive use of AnyView in SwiftUI erasing type information and disabling diff optimization
Core Data fetch requests without fetch limits or batch sizes loading entire dataset into memory
bestPractices: Use guard let/if let for safe Optional unwrapping — avoid force unwrap (!)
Use Result<Success, Failure> type for error-returning functions
Prefer value types (struct, enum) over reference types (class) where appropriate
Use weak/unowned references to break retain cycles in closures and delegates
Mark classes as final when subclassing is not intended (enables compiler optimizations)
Use Swift Concurrency (async/await, actors) instead of GCD for new code
Prefer Codable for JSON serialization instead of manual parsing
Use @MainActor for view models and UI-bound classes to ensure thread safety

swiftui: Missing @StateObject for owned observable objects (using @ObservedObject causes recreation on parent re-render)
Expensive computations in body property — extract to computed properties or cache in onAppear
Missing .task modifier for async loading — using .onAppear with Task {} does not auto-cancel
NavigationStack path not using NavigationPath for type-safe programmatic navigation
uikit: Missing dequeueReusableCell in UITableView/UICollectionView causing excessive cell allocation
Not invalidating layout on orientation change — constraints must adapt to size classes
Memory leaks from NotificationCenter observers not removed in deinit
vapor: Database queries not using async/await — blocking EventLoop threads causes thread starvation
Missing authentication middleware on protected routes
Returning raw SQL results without DTO mapping — leaks database schema to client

securityFocus: SQL injection in raw query strings (use parameterized queries with Slick/Doobie bind variables)
Hard-coded credentials or secrets in source code (use environment variables or config files excluded from VCS)
Unsafe deserialization of untrusted data (Java serialization or Kryo without class whitelisting)
Missing input validation on HTTP request parameters and body (use Play validators or custom extractors)
Command injection via Runtime.exec or Process with user-controlled arguments
XML External Entity (XXE) injection in Scala XML library (disable DOCTYPE processing)
SSRF when making HTTP requests with user-supplied URLs (validate scheme and host whitelist)
Insecure random number generation (use java.security.SecureRandom, not scala.util.Random for security)
Logging sensitive data — PII, tokens, or passwords logged via slf4j/logback
bugPatterns: Blocking a Future with Await.result in a thread pool context — causes thread starvation
Using null instead of Option[T] — prefer Option and pattern matching
Mutable shared state without synchronization (use Atomic, Actor model, or Ref from ZIO/Cats Effect)
Uncaught exceptions in Future — always attach .recover or .recoverWith
HTTP client created per request — reuse a shared client (Akka HTTP, sttp) with connection pool
Pattern match not exhaustive on sealed trait — missing cases cause MatchError at runtime
Implicit conversion hiding type errors — unexpected automatic conversions cause subtle bugs
Future callback executing on wrong ExecutionContext — blocking IO on computation pool
Using var in concurrent context without volatile or atomic — visibility issues across threads
Try.get without checking isSuccess — throws the original exception
performancePatterns: Blocking IO operations on Futures running on computation ExecutionContext (use blocking {} or separate IO pool)
Collecting entire DB resultset into memory instead of streaming (use Slick streaming/Reactive Streams/fs2)
Unnecessary serialization/deserialization in hot paths
Excessive object creation triggering GC pressure — use value classes or opaque types
Repeated collection traversals — chain operations or use lazy views
Not using parallel collections or Futures for independent CPU-bound tasks
String interpolation in tight loops creating intermediate String objects
bestPractices: Prefer Option[T] over null; use Either[Error, T] for error handling in functions
Use sealed traits for ADTs to enable exhaustive pattern matching
Handle all Future failures — a Future with no failure handling loses errors silently
Use case classes for immutable data with automatic equals/hashCode/copy
Avoid mutable state; prefer immutable collections and vals
Use for-comprehension for composing Futures, Options, or Either values
Prefer type classes over inheritance for polymorphism (ad-hoc polymorphism)
Use sbt-scalafmt for consistent formatting and scalafix for automated refactoring

play: Missing CSRF protection on state-changing routes (use CSRFCheck filter)
Action.async without recovery — unhandled Future failure returns 500 without logging
Blocking code in Action body — use play.api.libs.concurrent.CustomExecutionContext for IO
Hardcoded routes instead of using reverse routing (routes.controllers.*)
akka: Blocking inside Actor receive — use pipeTo pattern or separate dispatcher for IO
Actor not handling unknown messages — dead letters accumulate silently
Missing supervision strategy — child actor failures cause default restart behavior
cats-effect: Using IO.unsafeRunSync in production code — blocks the fiber scheduler
Resource not acquired with Resource.make — cleanup not guaranteed on failure
Missing error handling on IO — use handleErrorWith or attempt

securityFocus: SOQL injection — never concatenate user input into dynamic SOQL (use Database.query with bind variables or String.escapeSingleQuotes)
XSS in Visualforce controllers — escape output with HTMLENCODE() or JSENCODE() for JavaScript contexts
Hard-coded Salesforce IDs or credentials (use Custom Metadata Types, Custom Settings, or Named Credentials)
Missing 'with sharing' keyword — data visibility rules not enforced, user sees all records
Insecure direct object reference — missing ownership check or CRUD/FLS validation before DML on records
SOSL injection in dynamic search queries (use bind variables with Search.query)
Missing CRUD and FLS checks — Schema.sObjectType.Account.fields.Name.isAccessible() before field access
Open redirect in controller page references (validate URL before redirecting)
Exposing sensitive field data in debug logs — System.debug() output visible in production logs
bugPatterns: SOQL queries inside for loops — violates governor limits (max 100 SOQL per transaction)
DML operations inside for loops — violates governor limits (max 150 DML per transaction)
Hardcoded record type IDs or user IDs — not portable between orgs (query by DeveloperName)
Trigger without handler class — logic directly in trigger body is not testable or reusable
Missing null check on relationship fields accessed via dot notation (NullPointerException)
Map.get() without containsKey check — returns null and may cause NullPointerException downstream
Trigger.new vs Trigger.old confusion — using wrong context variable for before/after triggers
Missing Test.startTest()/Test.stopTest() around async operations in test methods
CPU timeout from inefficient loops or nested iterations over large record sets
Callout after DML in same transaction — causes CalloutException (reorder operations or use @future)
performancePatterns: Non-bulkified triggers — code must handle collections of records, not single records
Using Database.query when a static SOQL query would suffice (static SOQL is compiled and cached)
Querying more fields than needed in SOQL — select only required fields to reduce heap usage
Not using SOQL aggregate queries (COUNT, SUM) when only the aggregate value is needed
Recursive trigger execution without static flag guard — causes stack depth exception
Loading related records with subqueries instead of separate targeted queries when only parent data is needed
Not using Limits class to check remaining governor limits before expensive operations
bestPractices: Always use 'with sharing' unless 'without sharing' is explicitly required and documented
Write bulk-safe code — all DML and SOQL must operate on collections (List<SObject>)
Use trigger handler pattern (one trigger per object, delegate to handler class)
Achieve 100% test coverage with meaningful assertions (System.assertEquals, System.assertNotEquals)
Use Custom Metadata Types for configuration instead of hard-coded values
Use Platform Events or Queueable for async processing instead of @future where possible
Implement error handling with Database.SaveResult and partial success patterns
Follow Salesforce naming conventions — classes PascalCase, methods camelCase, constants UPPER_SNAKE

lightning: Apex controller methods missing @AuraEnabled annotation — not accessible from Lightning components
Missing cacheable=true on read-only @AuraEnabled methods — unnecessary server round trips
Returning too much data to Lightning components — causes heap size issues on client
Missing error handling in @AuraEnabled methods — exceptions surface as unhelpful 'Internal Server Error'
salesforce-api: Missing Namespace prefix on custom objects/fields in managed packages
Hardcoded API version in endpoint URLs — use dynamic versioning
Not handling API rate limits (429 responses) with retry logic

securityFocus: Format string vulnerabilities via Printf with user-controlled format string
Hard-coded credentials or secrets in source code
Insufficient input validation before processing external data
SQL injection in database queries using string concatenation (use parameterized queries with Caqti or PGOCaml)
Command injection via Unix.system or Unix.open_process with unsanitized user input
Deserialization of untrusted data with Marshal module — can execute arbitrary code (use Yojson/Sexplib instead)
Path traversal via Filename.concat with user-controlled path components (validate and sanitize paths)
Weak random number generation with Random module — use Mirage-crypto or Nocrypto for cryptographic randomness
Insecure temporary file creation — use Filename.temp_file instead of hand-crafted /tmp paths
bugPatterns: Non-exhaustive pattern matching — missing cases cause Match_failure at runtime (always match all constructors)
Integer overflow in arithmetic operations — OCaml int is 63-bit, use Zarith for big integers
Ignored exceptions from functions that return unit — use try/with for all effectful operations
Mutable state shared across threads without synchronization (use Mutex or Lwt_mutex)
Partial functions (List.hd, List.tl) on empty lists — use pattern matching or Option-returning alternatives
String comparison with = on Unicode — use proper Unicode normalization before comparison
Forgetting to flush output channels — use flush stdout or close_out for reliable output
Recursive module initialization causing undefined behavior — order initialization carefully
Catching all exceptions with _ in try/with — hides programming errors and assertion failures
performancePatterns: Non-tail-recursive functions on large lists — causes stack overflow (use tail-recursive variants with accumulator)
Excessive allocations from list concatenation with @ (use List.rev_append or Buffer for strings)
Repeated list traversals that could be combined into one pass (use fold for multiple computations)
Lazy values forced multiple times when result should be cached
Using List when Array would be more cache-friendly for random access patterns
Hashtbl without initial size hint — causes repeated resizing for large tables (provide ~size_hint)
String concatenation with ^ in loops — use Buffer.t for incremental string building
bestPractices: Handle all match cases explicitly — use _ wildcard only when truly intentional and add a comment
Use Result or option types for error handling instead of exceptions for control flow
Prefer immutable data structures — use mutable state only when performance requires it
Use the type system to enforce invariants at compile time (phantom types, private types)
Write .mli interface files for all public modules to control API surface
Use ppx_deriving for automatic generation of show, eq, ord, and sexp
Prefer Lwt or Async for concurrent IO instead of threads
Use dune build system with strict warnings enabled (-warn-error +a)

lwt: Lwt promise not awaited — fire-and-forget loses errors silently (use Lwt.async with error handler)
Blocking system calls inside Lwt — use Lwt_unix wrappers or Lwt_preemptive.detach
Missing Lwt.catch or Lwt.try_bind — unhandled promise rejection crashes the program
dream: Missing CSRF middleware on state-changing routes
SQL queries not using Dream.sql with parameterized placeholders
Missing error handler middleware — exceptions return raw stack traces to client

securityFocus: XSS via unescaped EL expressions (${user.name} — use fn:escapeXml or c:out with escapeXml='true')
SQL injection in scriptlets that build query strings by concatenation (use PreparedStatement)
Session fixation — not invalidating session on authentication (call request.getSession(false) and invalidate)
Sensitive data exposed in HTML comments (visible in page source — never put server-side info in HTML comments)
CSRF vulnerability — forms without hidden token field and server-side validation
Path traversal via include/forward with user-controlled path (validate and whitelist allowed paths)
Information disclosure through detailed error pages (configure custom error pages in web.xml)
Insecure direct object reference in request parameters — missing authorization before accessing resources
Hard-coded database credentials in JSP scriptlets or included configuration
bugPatterns: Missing null checks on EL expressions — NullPointerException at render time
Unclosed resources (Connection, Statement, ResultSet) in scriptlets — resource leak on every request
Business logic in JSP scriptlets instead of Servlet/Controller layer (violates MVC, untestable)
Incorrect JSTL tag library URI — tags silently rendered as text instead of executed
Missing page directive for content type — character encoding issues with non-ASCII content
Using request.getParameter() without null check — NullPointerException when parameter absent
Include directive (<%@ include %>) vs include action (<jsp:include>) confusion — static vs dynamic inclusion
Form action URL not encoded — special characters in parameters break form submission
Thread-safety issues in JSP instance variables — JSPs are singletons, shared across requests
performancePatterns: Heavy business logic or database queries directly in JSP (move to Servlet/Service layer)
Not using JSTL sql tags with connection pooling (configure DataSource in container, not per-page connections)
Synchronizing on JSP page object — serializes all requests to the page
Large JSP pages without fragment caching — entire page regenerated on each request
Scriptlets creating new objects on every request that could be shared or pooled (use application scope)
Excessive custom tag library instantiation — use tag pooling or simple tags for lightweight operations
bestPractices: Use JSTL (c:out, c:forEach, c:if) instead of scriptlets (<% %>) for all logic
Use c:out or fn:escapeXml for all user-provided output to prevent XSS
Follow MVC pattern — JSP only for presentation, no business logic or data access
Use JSP include directive for reusable fragments and static content
Configure custom error pages in web.xml instead of showing stack traces
Use EL (Expression Language) instead of scriptlet expressions (<%= %>)
Set page encoding explicitly: <%@ page contentType='text/html;charset=UTF-8' %>
Prefer JSP tag files over tag handler classes for simple reusable components

spring-mvc: Using JSP scriptlets instead of Spring form tags (form:input, form:errors) for data binding
Missing BindingResult parameter after @ModelAttribute — validation errors cause exception instead of error page
Not using Spring Security taglib (sec:authorize) for conditional rendering based on roles
struts: Action form not validated — missing validate() method or validation.xml configuration
Using Struts 1 html:form without proper token handling for CSRF protection
Hardcoded forward names instead of using action mapping constants

securityFocus: XSS via apex:outputText or apex:outputLabel with escape='false' (default is true — do not override)
Exposing sensitive record fields without checking user permissions in controller
SOQL injection in controller methods backing VF components (use bind variables)
bugPatterns: Controller action methods not checking record ownership before DML
Missing null check on controller properties accessed in VF expressions
Large data sets rendered without pagination — causes view state and memory issues
View state too large — store large data in transient variables
performancePatterns: Fetching all records without LIMIT in controller SOQL (use pagination / StandardSetController)
Non-bulkified controller actions performing DML inside loops
Expensive operations in getter methods (called multiple times per page render — cache result)
bestPractices: Always use escape='true' on outputText (it is the default — do not override to false)
Use 'with sharing' on all Apex controllers
Use StandardSetController for list views with pagination
Avoid inline SOQL in getters — query once in constructor or init action

securityFocus: Hard-coded credentials, passwords, or secrets in .tf or .tfvars files (use Vault, environment variables, or cloud secret manager data sources)
Overly permissive IAM policies — wildcard actions (*) or wildcard resources (*) in production
Public network access to databases or storage — ensure databases are not publicly accessible
Unencrypted storage — S3 buckets, RDS, EBS volumes should have encryption enabled
S3 bucket without block_public_access settings — can expose data publicly
Security group with wide port ranges open to 0.0.0.0/0 — overly permissive ingress
Missing deletion protection on production databases (deletion_protection = false)
Sensitive output values exposed without sensitive = true flag
bugPatterns: Provider version not pinned — required_providers version constraint missing, breaks on provider update
Missing required_providers block — implicit provider version causes inconsistency across environments
Missing depends_on for implicit resource dependencies not captured by references
count used instead of for_each for resources with keys — causes ordering issues on removal
Referencing data source that may not exist without a lifecycle precondition
Missing backend configuration — state stored locally is not suitable for team use
performancePatterns: Data source queried on every plan when result is stable — use locals or outputs to cache
Large number of resources in a single state file — split into modules/workspaces to reduce blast radius
Missing lifecycle { create_before_destroy = true } for resources that cause downtime on replacement
bestPractices: Use remote state backend with state locking (S3 + DynamoDB, Terraform Cloud, GitLab-managed)
Pin all provider versions with ~> X.Y
Use workspaces or separate state files for environments (dev/staging/prod)
Run terraform fmt and terraform validate in CI pipeline
Use tflint and tfsec/checkov for security scanning
Mark sensitive variables with sensitive = true to prevent leakage in plan output
Use modules for reusable infrastructure patterns

securityFocus: Running as root — always add USER instruction with a non-root user before CMD/ENTRYPOINT
Secrets in ENV or ARG instructions — visible in image layers and docker history (use build secrets or runtime injection)
Using ADD with remote URLs instead of COPY + RUN curl with checksum verification
Exposing unnecessary ports in EXPOSE — only expose what is needed
Installing packages without version pinning — not reproducible (pin versions: apt-get install curl=7.x)
bugPatterns: Using :latest tag for base image — not reproducible, can silently break builds (use specific version or digest)
Running apt-get update in a separate RUN from apt-get install — stale package index in cached layer
COPY . . before installing dependencies — invalidates dependency cache on every code change
Missing .dockerignore file — copies .git, node_modules, secrets into image
CMD as shell string instead of JSON array — PID 1 is shell, not app (signals not forwarded properly)
performancePatterns: Not using multi-stage builds — final image includes build tools, compilers, dev dependencies
Missing --no-install-recommends on apt-get install — installs unnecessary packages
Copying large directories unnecessarily before filtering (rely on .dockerignore)
Not combining RUN commands — creates unnecessary layers increasing image size
bestPractices: Use specific base image versions with digest pinning (FROM node:20.11.0-alpine)
Add HEALTHCHECK instruction for service containers
Run as non-root user (addgroup + adduser or useradd, then USER instruction)
Combine apt-get update and install in single RUN and clean up apt lists afterward
Use multi-stage builds to minimize final image size
Order instructions from least to most frequently changed to maximize layer cache hits

securityFocus: Privileged containers (privileged: true) — grants all Linux capabilities to container
Port bindings to all interfaces (- '5432:5432' exposes to network — use '127.0.0.1:5432:5432' for local-only services)
Secrets as plain-text environment variables — use Docker secrets or external secret manager
Missing read-only filesystem (read_only: true) for containers that do not need to write to disk
Mounting Docker socket (/var/run/docker.sock) — allows container to escape to host
bugPatterns: Missing depends_on with condition: service_healthy — service starts before dependency is ready
Hard-coded volume host paths — not portable across environments (use named volumes)
Missing restart policy — containers do not recover from crashes (add restart: unless-stopped)
Services sharing a network with more access than needed — use separate networks per service group
performancePatterns: Missing resource limits (deploy.resources.limits for memory and CPU) — single container can starve host
No health check defined for depends_on — compose cannot wait for readiness properly
Logging driver not configured — logs accumulate unbounded on host filesystem
bestPractices: Use named volumes instead of bind mounts for persistent data
Use .env file for environment-specific configuration (never commit .env with secrets)
Define resource limits for all services in production-like environments
Use healthcheck for all stateful services (databases, caches)
Use specific image versions, not :latest

securityFocus: Hard-coded credentials or tokens in script steps — use CI/CD masked variables
Pulling and running unverified external scripts (curl https://... | bash) — verify checksums or use internal mirrors
Missing secret masking — variables containing secrets must be marked as Masked in CI/CD settings
Overly permissive job rules (when: always on protected branches) — require manual approval for production deployments
Using shell executor with insufficient isolation — prefer Docker executor
Forked pipeline access to parent project secrets — restrict CI variables to protected branches only
Artifacts containing secrets or credentials — mark sensitive files as excluded from artifacts
Missing SAST/secret-detection jobs — vulnerable code and leaked secrets reach production
Insecure Docker-in-Docker (dind) without TLS — use DOCKER_TLS_CERTDIR for encrypted communication
bugPatterns: Missing set -e in script blocks — errors silently ignored and job reports success
Cache key not including lock file — stale dependencies served from cache after package updates
Artifact paths do not match actual output paths — artifacts silently empty
Wrong stage ordering causing jobs to run before their dependencies complete
Missing needs or dependencies declaration — jobs wait unnecessarily in sequential stages
Variable expansion in wrong context — $CI_COMMIT_SHA empty in extends or include templates
Rules with conflicting conditions — only: and except: combined with rules: causes validation error
Missing allow_failure: true on optional quality gates — flaky lint blocks deployment
Incorrect when: condition in rules — job runs on wrong events (push vs merge_request)
performancePatterns: Downloading dependencies on every job without cache — use cache: with correct key and paths
Not parallelizing independent jobs (test matrix, linting, security scan can run in parallel)
Uploading large artifacts that are not consumed by downstream jobs
Not using extends or !reference for shared job templates — duplicated configuration
Missing interruptible: true on jobs for superseded pipelines — old pipelines consume runner capacity
Docker image pulled on every job — use private registry mirror or pre-pulled images on runners
Not using parallel: keyword for splitting large test suites across multiple jobs
bestPractices: Pin image tags with digest for reproducibility
Use extends or !reference for reusable job templates
Set timeout on jobs to prevent hung jobs from blocking runners indefinitely
Use needs: [] for DAG pipeline to skip unnecessary stage waits
Use environments with protection rules for production deployments
Run SAST and secret detection jobs on all merge requests
Use include: with remote or project templates for shared pipeline configuration
Add retry: configuration for jobs affected by transient infrastructure failures

securityFocus: Hard-coded secrets in workflow files — use GitHub encrypted secrets (${{ secrets.TOKEN }})
Using pull_request_target with checkout of PR head — allows fork PRs to access repository secrets
Script injection via untrusted expressions (${{ github.event.issue.title }}) in run: blocks — use environment variables instead
Third-party actions pinned by tag instead of commit SHA — tags can be moved to inject malicious code
Workflow triggered by pull_request from forks accessing secrets — use pull_request_target with caution
GITHUB_TOKEN with excessive permissions — set minimum permissions: block at workflow level
Artifact upload containing secrets or credentials — artifacts are accessible to anyone with repo read access
Self-hosted runner without isolation — fork PRs can execute arbitrary code on runner host
Missing environment protection rules for production deployment workflows
bugPatterns: Missing shell specification in run: steps — defaults vary by runner OS (use shell: bash explicitly)
Incorrect if: condition syntax — expressions must use ${{ }} wrapper for GitHub context
needs: referencing non-existent job ID — workflow fails at parse time
Matrix strategy without fail-fast: false — one failing combination cancels all other matrix jobs
Artifact name collision between jobs — later upload overwrites earlier artifact silently
Missing continue-on-error for non-critical steps — optional checks block the entire workflow
Checkout with wrong ref for pull request — actions/checkout defaults to merge commit, not PR head
Cache key not including lockfile hash — stale dependencies served from cache
Timeout not set on jobs — stuck jobs consume runner minutes indefinitely (set timeout-minutes:)
performancePatterns: Not caching dependencies between runs — use actions/cache or setup-* actions with built-in caching
Sequential jobs that could run in parallel — structure jobs with proper needs: dependencies
Downloading large artifacts in every job — scope artifact downloads to jobs that need them
Running full test suite on every push — use path filters to skip irrelevant workflows
Not using concurrency: groups to cancel superseded workflow runs
Building Docker images without layer caching — use docker/build-push-action with cache-from/cache-to
Checkout with full history (fetch-depth: 0) when only latest commit is needed
bestPractices: Pin all third-party actions to full commit SHA (uses: actions/checkout@abcdef123)
Set minimum GITHUB_TOKEN permissions at workflow level (permissions: block)
Use reusable workflows (workflow_call) for shared CI/CD logic across repositories
Add timeout-minutes to all jobs to prevent runaway billing
Use concurrency groups with cancel-in-progress for PR workflows
Use environment protection rules with required reviewers for production deployments
Add status badges and branch protection rules requiring workflow success
Use composite actions for reusable multi-step logic within a repository

securityFocus: Hard-coded secrets in script steps — use secret pipeline variables or Azure Key Vault task
Missing secret masking — all sensitive variables must be marked as Secret in pipeline settings
Overly permissive service connections — scope to specific resource groups/subscriptions
Missing environment approvals for production deployments — add approval gates on environments
Using self-hosted agents for untrusted fork PR builds — fork PRs can access agent environment
bugPatterns: Missing dependsOn for pipeline stages — stages may run in wrong order
Condition expression referencing undefined variable — evaluates to empty string silently
Incorrect artifact download path — DownloadPipelineArtifact without explicit path causes confusion
continueOnError: true on critical steps — failures silently ignored and pipeline reports success
Missing checkout: self step in jobs that need source code
performancePatterns: Not caching dependencies between pipeline runs (NuGet, npm, pip cache tasks available)
Downloading unnecessary artifacts in every job — scope artifact downloads to jobs that need them
Sequential stages for independent workloads — use parallel jobs within a stage
bestPractices: Use pipeline templates (extends, template references) for reusable steps and policies
Pin task versions (e.g., DotNetCoreCLI@2) to avoid unexpected behavior after task updates
Use variable groups for shared configuration across pipelines
Store secrets in Azure Key Vault and reference via variable group + Key Vault link
Add timeout to jobs and steps to prevent indefinitely hanging pipelines

securityFocus: Hard-coded credentials, API keys, or secrets in YAML config files (use environment variables or secret managers)
Sensitive data in Kubernetes ConfigMaps or Secrets YAML files committed to repository
Overly permissive RBAC rules in Kubernetes YAML (cluster-admin bindings, wildcard permissions)
Insecure container image references using :latest tag or untrusted registries
Missing resource limits in Kubernetes pod specs — potential DoS via resource exhaustion
Privileged containers in Kubernetes YAML (privileged: true, hostPID, hostNetwork)
Secrets mounted as environment variables instead of files (env vars may be exposed in process list)
bugPatterns: Incorrect indentation in YAML — causes parsing errors or unexpected structure
Using tabs instead of spaces for indentation — invalid YAML
Duplicate keys in YAML mappings — later value silently overwrites earlier
Unquoted strings that are interpreted as other types (yes/no/on/off as booleans)
Missing quotes around strings containing special characters (:, #, {, }, [, ], ,, &, *, ?, |, -, <, >, =, !, %, @, `)
Multi-line strings without proper chomping indicator (|- vs |) causing unexpected newlines
Anchor (&) without alias (*) or alias referencing non-existent anchor
Incorrect use of YAML anchors and aliases causing circular references
Numeric version strings parsed as numbers (1.10 becomes 1.1) — quote version strings
performancePatterns: Loading large YAML files entirely into memory — use streaming YAML parsers for large datasets
Deeply nested YAML structures that are hard to parse and query — flatten where possible
Redundant data duplication across YAML config files — use YAML anchors or shared templates
bestPractices: Use YAML Schema validation for configuration files to catch errors at build time
Keep YAML config files free of secrets — use environment-specific overrides or secret managers
Use consistent indentation (2 spaces) enforced by yamllint or similar tool
Quote all strings to avoid type coercion surprises
Validate YAML files in CI pipeline to catch syntax errors before deployment
Use anchors and aliases for DRY configuration, but document complex references

securityFocus: Hard-coded credentials or tokens in CI configuration files — use CI-native secret management
Overly permissive pipeline permissions granting access to sensitive environments
Missing branch protection rules allowing direct pushes to main/production branches
Fork PR builds accessing repository secrets without approval workflow
Self-hosted runners without proper isolation for untrusted code
Missing secret masking in pipeline logs — secrets visible in build output
bugPatterns: Missing error handling in CI scripts — failures silently ignored
Incorrect path filters causing pipelines to run on irrelevant changes
Cache configuration not including lock files — stale dependencies
Missing timeout on jobs — stuck pipelines consume resources indefinitely
Race conditions in parallel jobs accessing shared resources
Incorrect environment variable scoping — variables not available where expected
performancePatterns: Downloading dependencies on every build without caching
Running full test suites when only linting is needed for certain changes
Not parallelizing independent jobs — sequential execution wastes time
Large artifact uploads/downloads that are not consumed by downstream jobs
Not using concurrency controls to cancel outdated pipeline runs
bestPractices: Use pipeline templates for reusable CI/CD patterns
Pin all tool and action versions for reproducible builds
Set timeouts on all jobs to prevent runaway resource consumption
Use path filters to skip unnecessary pipeline runs
Require manual approval for production deployments
Run security scans (SAST, dependency check) in all pipelines
Use concurrency groups to cancel superseded runs

securityFocus: Hard-coded credentials, API keys, or connection strings in JSON config files (use environment variables or secret managers)
Sensitive default values in example/template JSON files committed to repository
Database connection strings with embedded passwords in appsettings.json or config.json
OAuth client secrets or private keys stored in JSON configuration
Overly permissive CORS origins configured in JSON (avoid wildcard * with credentials)
JWT signing keys or HMAC secrets in JSON configuration files
Cloud provider access keys (AWS, GCP, Azure) in JSON service account or credential files
Unmasked PII or test data with real user information in JSON fixtures or seed files
bugPatterns: Trailing commas in JSON (not valid per spec — causes parse failure in strict parsers)
Incorrect data types for configuration values (string '80' instead of number 80 for port)
Duplicate keys in JSON objects — later value silently overwrites earlier (behavior varies by parser)
Comments in JSON (not valid per spec — use JSON5 or JSONC if comments are needed)
Missing required fields in configuration — application fails at runtime with cryptic error
Incorrect JSON escape sequences (\n vs literal newline, \u vs Unicode escape)
Deeply nested structures exceeding parser depth limits — causes stack overflow in some parsers
BOM (Byte Order Mark) at start of JSON file — causes parse failure in some implementations
Numeric precision loss for large integers (JSON numbers > 2^53 lose precision in JavaScript)
performancePatterns: Extremely large JSON files loaded entirely into memory — use streaming JSON parsers (SAX-style) for large datasets
Redundant data duplication across JSON config files — use shared base config with environment overrides
Deeply nested JSON structures that are hard to parse and query — flatten where possible
Large JSON payloads in API responses without pagination — causes client memory issues
Uncompressed JSON in network transfer — enable gzip/brotli compression for JSON API responses
JSON used for binary data (base64-encoded) — consider binary formats (protobuf, msgpack) for large payloads
bestPractices: Use JSON Schema validation for configuration files to catch errors at build time
Keep JSON config files free of secrets — use environment-specific overrides outside the repo
Use consistent formatting (2-space or 4-space indent) enforced by prettier or similar tool
Add $schema reference for IDE autocompletion and validation
Use .example.json files with placeholder values for configuration templates
Validate JSON files in CI pipeline to catch syntax errors before deployment
Use JSONC or JSON5 for files that benefit from comments (tsconfig.json, devcontainer.json)
Keep JSON API payloads flat and avoid unnecessary nesting for easier consumption

securityFocus: XXE (XML External Entity) injection — DOCTYPE declarations with SYSTEM or PUBLIC entities
Sensitive data or credentials embedded in XML configuration files
XPath injection when building XPath queries from user input
bugPatterns: Missing XML namespace declarations causing parser failures
Encoding declaration mismatch between XML header and actual file encoding
performancePatterns: Loading entire large XML document into DOM (use SAX or StAX streaming parser for large files)
bestPractices: Disable DOCTYPE/DTD processing in XML parsers to prevent XXE
Use XML Schema (XSD) validation for configuration and data exchange files

securityFocus: Command injection via unquoted variables (always double-quote variable expansions: "$var")
Path traversal through unsanitized file paths
Unsafe evaluation of dynamic content (avoid eval on any external or user-controlled input)
Hard-coded credentials and API keys in scripts
Improper temporary file creation (use mktemp, never predictable names in /tmp)
Unsafe pipe-to-shell patterns — verify checksums or use package managers
Word splitting on unquoted variables causing unexpected behavior
Insecure use of sudo without input validation
Sensitive data passed via command-line arguments (visible in ps output — use environment variables or stdin)
Permissions on script files too open (chmod 777) — use least-privilege permissions (755 for scripts, 600 for configs)
bugPatterns: Missing error handling — use set -euo pipefail at script start
Uninitialized variables causing silent empty string substitution (use set -u or default: ${var:-default})
Incorrect test syntax ([ ] vs [[ ]] in Bash) — prefer [[ ]] for string comparisons
Missing shebang line — interpreter undefined
cd without error handling — subsequent commands run in wrong directory if cd fails
Using $? after multiple commands — $? only captures last command exit code
Missing quotes around variables with spaces causing word splitting and globbing
Arithmetic overflow in $(( )) expressions — Bash integers are 64-bit signed, no overflow detection
Using == inside [ ] instead of = for POSIX compatibility — [ ] uses single = for string comparison
Here-document delimiter not quoted — variables expanded unexpectedly (use <<'EOF' to prevent expansion)
performancePatterns: Unnecessary subshells and command substitutions in tight loops
Useless use of cat (cat file | grep — use grep directly with file argument)
Multiple grep/sed/awk in a pipeline when single awk command would suffice
Forking external commands in tight loops (use Bash built-ins where possible)
Reading file line-by-line with while read loop when awk/sed could process entire file at once
Not using GNU parallel or xargs -P for embarrassingly parallel operations
bestPractices: Always use shebang (#!/usr/bin/env bash or #!/bin/sh for portability)
Enable strict mode: set -euo pipefail
Quote all variable expansions: "$var", "$@", "${array[@]}"
Use local for function-scoped variables
Prefer [[ ]] over [ ] for conditionals in Bash
Use $() instead of backticks for command substitution
Validate all external inputs before use
Use ShellCheck for static analysis

makefiles: Missing .PHONY declarations for non-file targets — make skips target if file with same name exists
Using shell variables without $$ escaping — make expands single $ as make variable
Missing error handling in recipe commands — prefix with set -e or use .SHELLFLAGS
docker-entrypoint: Missing exec before final command — shell remains PID 1, signals not forwarded to application
Not handling SIGTERM gracefully — container takes 10s to stop (Docker sends SIGKILL after timeout)
Environment variable substitution in config files without envsubst or sed validation
